to developing overall well-being. It includes physical, social, emotional, spiritual, and mental
activities: INTRODUCTION
Fitness goes beyond physical abilities like sit-ups or running. It’s about the body’s systems
working efficiently to meet every day’s demands and adapt to stress. Fitness connects closely
to developing overall well-being. It includes physical, social, emotional, spiritual, and mental

challenges, whether academic or athletic. This perspective teaches learners that total fitness
contributes to success in both sports and daily life.

activities: Physical Education and make a significant contribution to the set goals.
Gymnastics involves systematic and rhythmic exercises on the floor (ground), bars and apparatus.

lessons.title: 1.7 RECREATIONAL GAMES AND ACTIVITIES
topics: INTRODUCTION
Recreational games are a structured form of play, usually undertaken for enjoyment, relaxation,
and sometimes competition. They can be physical, mental, or a combination of both, and are
activities: INTRODUCTION
Recreational games are a structured form of play, usually undertaken for enjoyment, relaxation,
and sometimes competition. They can be physical, mental, or a combination of both, and are
primarily for fun, fitness, relaxation, and social interaction rather than for serious competition.
They are an important part of physical education and holistic development, providing opportunities
for learners to engage in enjoyable movement-based experiences.
development. They serve as a stress reliever and create an engaging way for individuals to stay
active and healthy. Recreational games also help foster essential life skills such as teamwork,

lessons.title: 1.8 SWIMMING
topics: INTRODUCTION
Swimming refers to the act of moving through water using coordinated movements of the arms
and legs typically employing specific strokes. It can also describe gliding or floating effortlessly
on the water’s surface. Throughout history, swimming has been an important survival skill and a
activities: In modern times, swimming is widely recognized for its many benefits. It provides a full-body
workout, improving cardiovascular endurance, muscle strength, flexibility and coordination.
Whether enjoyed for fun with friends and family or pursued as a competitive sport, swimming
promotes both physical and mental well-being.
This topic focuses on introducing learners to the fundamentals of swimming with special attention
to water safety practices and acceptable pool hygiene.
General Competencies to be attained by learners:
Creativity and Innovation: Applying knowledge and skills in new and creative ways.
